Bash(cd /Users/liamharvey/Desktop/Gallus\\ Project && python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Load the data
df = pd.read_csv\('data/sectG.csv'\)

# Define target columns
lock_cols = ['Rate Lock D', 'Rate Lock Expiration D', 'LockDurationDays', 'HasRateLock', 'Rate Lock Status', 'Lock Period \(days\)']

print\("=" * 80\)
print\("LOCK-RELATED COLUMNS: Data Types & Coverage"\)
print\("=" * 80\)

for col in lock_cols:
    if col in df.columns:
        print\(f"\\n{col}:"\)
        print\(f"  Data type: {df[col].dtype}"\)
        print\(f"  Non-null count: {df[col].notna\(\).sum\(\)} / {len\(df\)} \({100*df[col].notna\(\).sum\(\)/len\(df\):.1f}%\)"\)
        print\(f"  Null count: {df[col].isna\(\).sum\(\)}"\)
        
        # Sample values
        non_null_samples = df[col].dropna\(\).head\(3\).tolist\(\)
        print\(f"  Sample values: {non_null_samples}"\)
        
        # Distribution for key columns
        if col == 'Rate Lock Status':
            print\(f"  Value distribution:"\)
            vc = df[col].value_counts\(\)
            for val, count in vc.items\(\):
                print\(f"    {val}: {count} \({100*count/len\(df\):.1f}%\)"\)
        elif col == 'HasRateLock':
            print\(f"  Value distribution:"\)
            vc = df[col].value_counts\(\)
            for val, count in vc.items\(\):
                print\(f"    {val}: {count} \({100*count/len\(df\):.1f}%\)"\)
        elif col == 'LockDurationDays':
            numeric_vals = pd.to_numeric\(df[col], errors='coerce'\)
            print\(f"  Stats: mean={numeric_vals.mean\(\):.1f}, median={numeric_vals.median\(\):.1f}, min={numeric_vals.min\(\):.1f}, max={numeric_vals.max\(\):.1f}"\)
            print\(f"  Quartiles \(25%, 50%, 75%\): {numeric_vals.quantile\(0.25\):.1f}, {numeric_vals.quantile\(0.50\):.1f}, {numeric_vals.quantile\(0.75\):.1f}"\)
        elif col == 'Lock Period \(days\)':
            numeric_vals = pd.to_numeric\(df[col], errors='coerce'\)
            print\(f"  Stats: mean={numeric_vals.mean\(\):.1f}, median={numeric_vals.median\(\):.1f}, min={numeric_vals.min\(\):.1f}, max={numeric_vals.max\(\):.1f}"\)
            print\(f"  Quartiles \(25%, 50%, 75%\): {numeric_vals.quantile\(0.25\):.1f}, {numeric_vals.quantile\(0.50\):.1f}, {numeric_vals.quantile\(0.75\):.1f}"\)

print\("\\n" + "=" * 80\)
print\("FUNDING DEFINITION & HasRateLock Cross-Tab"\)
print\("=" * 80\)

# Define funded: Funded D is non-null
df['DidFund'] = df['Funded D'].notna\(\)
print\(f"\\nFunded loans \(Funded D non-null\): {df['DidFund'].sum\(\)} / {len\(df\)} \({100*df['DidFund'].sum\(\)/len\(df\):.1f}%\)"\)
print\(f"Non-funded: {\(~df['DidFund']\).sum\(\)}"\)

# Cross-tab: HasRateLock vs DidFund
print\("\\nCross-tab: HasRateLock vs DidFund"\)
crosstab = pd.crosstab\(df['HasRateLock'], df['DidFund'], margins=True\)
print\(crosstab\)

# Calculate funding rate by HasRateLock
print\("\\nFunding rate by HasRateLock:"\)
for val in [0, 1]:
    subset = df[df['HasRateLock'] == val]
    funded_rate = subset['DidFund'].sum\(\) / len\(subset\) if len\(subset\) > 0 else 0
    print\(f"  HasRateLock={val}: {funded_rate*100:.1f}% \({subset['DidFund'].sum\(\)} / {len\(subset\)}\)"\)

# Cross-tab: Rate Lock Status vs DidFund
print\("\\nCross-tab: Rate Lock Status vs DidFund"\)
crosstab2 = pd.crosstab\(df['Rate Lock Status'], df['DidFund'], margins=True\)
print\(crosstab2\)

print\("\\nFunding rate by Rate Lock Status:"\)
for status in df['Rate Lock Status'].dropna\(\).unique\(\):
    subset = df[df['Rate Lock Status'] == status]
    funded_rate = subset['DidFund'].sum\(\) / len\(subset\) if len\(subset\) > 0 else 0
    print\(f"  {status}: {funded_rate*100:.1f}% \({subset['DidFund'].sum\(\)} / {len\(subset\)}\)"\)

EOF)

Bash(cd /Users/liamharvey/Desktop/Gallus\\ Project && python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd

# Read backtest results and filter for Nov 2025 and Dec 2025
df = pd.read_csv\('outputs/results/backtest_results.csv'\)

# Nov 2025
print\("=" * 80\)
print\("NOVEMBER 2025 BACKTEST RESULTS"\)
print\("=" * 80\)
nov = df[\(df['year'] == 2025\) & \(df['month'] == 11\)].copy\(\)
nov_pivot = nov.pivot_table\(
    index='snapshot_day', 
    columns='method', 
    values='error_pct'
\)
print\("\\nMAPE by snapshot_day and method:"\)
print\(nov_pivot\)

# Show all Nov rows with full columns
print\("\\nFull rows \(Nov 2025\):"\)
for _, row in nov.iterrows\(\):
    print\(f"Day {row['snapshot_day']:2d} | {row['method']:20s} | "
          f"Projected: ${row['projected']:15.0f} | Actual: ${row['actual']:15.0f} | "
          f"Error: {row['error_pct']:7.2f}%"\)

print\("\\n" + "=" * 80\)
print\("DECEMBER 2025 BACKTEST RESULTS"\)
print\("=" * 80\)
dec = df[\(df['year'] == 2025\) & \(df['month'] == 12\)].copy\(\)
dec_pivot = dec.pivot_table\(
    index='snapshot_day', 
    columns='method', 
    values='error_pct'
\)
print\("\\nMAPE by snapshot_day and method:"\)
print\(dec_pivot\)

# Show all Dec rows with full columns
print\("\\nFull rows \(Dec 2025\):"\)
for _, row in dec.iterrows\(\):
    print\(f"Day {row['snapshot_day']:2d} | {row['method']:20s} | "
          f"Projected: ${row['projected']:15.0f} | Actual: ${row['actual']:15.0f} | "
          f"Error: {row['error_pct']:7.2f}%"\)

# Summary statistics for comparison
print\("\\n" + "=" * 80\)
print\("SUMMARY: Nov & Dec 2025 MAPE by Method"\)
print\("=" * 80\)
all_nov_dec = df[\(\(df['year'] == 2025\) & \(\(df['month'] == 11\) | \(df['month'] == 12\)\)\)]
for method in ['Transition Tables', 'ML']:
    subset = all_nov_dec[all_nov_dec['method'] == method]
    print\(f"\\n{method}:"\)
    print\(f"  Mean MAPE: {subset['error_pct'].abs\(\).mean\(\):.2f}%"\)
    print\(f"  Median MAPE: {subset['error_pct'].abs\(\).median\(\):.2f}%"\)
    print\(f"  Min error: {subset['error_pct'].min\(\):.2f}%"\)
    print\(f"  Max error: {subset['error_pct'].max\(\):.2f}%"\)

EOF)

Bash(cd /Users/liamharvey/Desktop/Gallus\\ Project && python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd

# Summary of lock-related data leakage risk
print\("=" * 80\)
print\("DATA LEAKAGE RISK ANALYSIS: Lock-Related Features"\)
print\("=" * 80\)

df = pd.read_csv\('data/sectG.csv'\)
df['DidFund'] = df['Funded D'].notna\(\)

# Calculate the leakage risk indicators
has_lock_not_funded = df[\(df['HasRateLock'] == 0\) & \(df['DidFund'] == False\)].shape[0]
has_lock_funded = df[\(df['HasRateLock'] == 1\) & \(df['DidFund'] == True\)].shape[0]

print\("\\nLEAKAGE RISK ASSESSMENT:"\)
print\("-" * 80\)

print\("\\n1. RATE LOCK STATUS / HAS RATE LOCK"\)
print\(f"   Funding rate if NOT locked: 0.5% \(41 / 7,873\)"\)
print\(f"   Funding rate if locked:     79.5% \(5,620 / 7,399\)"\)
print\(f"   Spread: 79.0 percentage points"\)
print\(f"\\n   → This is EXTREMELY predictive but HIGHLY LEAKY"\)
print\(f"     Reason: Whether a loan is locked is a CONSEQUENCE of it progressing,"\)
print\(f"     not a CAUSE. Loans get locked when lenders believe they'll fund."\)
print\(f"     It's a lagging indicator, not a leading one."\)

print\("\\n2. LOCK PERIOD \(DAYS\)"\)
print\(f"   Feature: 'Lock Period \(days\)' column exists and is 100% populated"\)
print\(f"   Mean: 23.5 days | Median: 30 days | Range: 0-85 days"\)
print\(f"   Contains: Lock period duration as of origination/snapshot"\)
print\(f"\\n   → MORE LEGITIMATE than HasRateLock for predictive use"\)
print\(f"     Reason: Lock period is set at origination and doesn't change"\)
print\(f"     based on the outcome. It's a product feature, not a consequence."\)

print\("\\n3. LOCK DURATION DAYS"\)
print\(f"   Availability: 48.2% of loans \(7,361 / 15,272\)"\)
print\(f"   Meaning: Calendar days between Rate Lock D and Rate Lock Expiration D"\)
print\(f"   Mean: 30.7 | Median: 30 | Range: 3-85 days"\)
print\(f"\\n   → POTENTIALLY LEAKY if computed AFTER rate lock decision"\)
print\(f"     Only available for ~48% of loans → missing not random"\)

print\("\\n4. RATE LOCK D & RATE LOCK EXPIRATION D"\)
print\(f"   Coverage: 48.2-48.4% of loans"\)
print\(f"\\n   → TIMING DEPENDENT for leakage"\)
print\(f"     If measured BEFORE prediction date: OK \(leading\)"\)
print\(f"     If measured AFTER prediction date: LEAKY \(lagging\)"\)

print\("\\n" + "=" * 80\)
print\("RECOMMENDATION FOR v2 MODEL"\)
print\("=" * 80\)
print\("""
KEEP:
  - Lock Period \(days\): The static lock duration set at origination
    Correlation with funding: 0.4172 \(strongest numeric feature!\)
    No leakage risk: set at origination, doesn't change based on outcome
    
DO NOT USE directly:
  - HasRateLock / Rate Lock Status: 0.5% vs 79.5% split is outcome-proxying
    These are consequences of pipeline progression, not drivers
    Can include as a FEATURE INTERACTION but understand the leakage

  - LockDurationDays: Only available for ~48%, computed AFTER lock date
    Introduces selection bias \(missing values non-random\)
    
HYBRID APPROACH for v2:
  1. Keep Lock Period \(days\) — it's the most legitimate
  2. If you want to use HasRateLock, treat it as a PIPELINE STAGE variable
     \(e.g., "has_lock_x_stage" interaction\) rather than a standalone predictor
  3. Consider time-to-lock expiration: 
     If Lock Period is 30 days, and you have 20 days left in month,
     that's only 10 days of cushion — higher risk of not funding
"""\)

EOF)
